cuisine: Malaysian commercial / Singaporean mass-market packaged snack
region_of_origin: Malaysia (manufactured by Calbee-Mamee Sdn Bhd); widely distributed in Singapore
food_subgroup: Sliced fried potato crisps — continuous-process or kettle-style; single-serve pack 55 g

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 covers potato chips generically; no branded Mr Potato entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers potato crisps generically; no branded variant code available.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for branded potato crisps product.

preparation_method: seasoned with salt, onion powder, garlic powder

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard single-serve pack of Mr Potato Original Flavour Potato Crisps weighs 55 g and is typically consumed in one sitting, providing approximately 290 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Mr Potato Original Potato Crisps — Nutrition Information Panel (NIP), Singapore product label; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Potato chips, plain, salted (FDC ID 19411)
nutrient_source.organization: Calbee-Mamee Sdn Bhd (primary label data); USDA Agricultural Research Service (cross-reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values sourced from the Calbee-Mamee Singapore product NIP (verified label). Micronutrient values (potassium, magnesium, phosphorus, B-vitamins, selenium) cross-referenced and validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain salted potato chips (FDC ID 19411). Values are consistent with the product label within expected analytical variance. USDA FDC is an approved secondary source under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of potato chips / crisps — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~75 (high) sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for regular fried potato crisps/chips. Mr Potato Original uses sliced fried potato in palm olein — directly analogous to the tested product category. Glycemic load per 55 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (75 × 34.1) / 100 ≈ 25.6. Available carbohydrate per 55 g = 62.0 × 0.55 = 34.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — 545 mg/100 g; 300 mg per 55 g single-serve pack (15% of the 2,000 mg/day recommended limit)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Mr Potato Original Flavour Potato Crisps; a single 55 g pack delivers 300 mg sodium, and regular consumption will substantially contribute to exceeding the 2,000 mg/day sodium ceiling, despite the partial counter-effect of the high potassium content (467 mg/pack) from real potato.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~75) and high glycemic load (~26 per 55 g pack) combined with high energy density (290 kcal/pack)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat Mr Potato Original Flavour Potato Crisps as an occasional indulgence only; the GI of ~75 and GL of ~26 per pack will produce a significant postprandial glucose spike, and the 16.5 g fat per pack, while moderating the glucose peak slightly, adds substantial caloric load that complicates glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from palm olein (14 g/100 g; 7.7 g per 55 g pack) and high sodium (300 mg/pack)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of Mr Potato Original Flavour Potato Crisps; the saturated fat content (14 g/100 g from palm olein) raises LDL cholesterol risk, and the combined sodium and saturated fat load makes this snack unfavourable for cardiovascular health when consumed regularly.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat, high GI, and acrylamide from high-temperature frying
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption of Mr Potato Original Flavour Potato Crisps; the high saturated fat content promotes pro-inflammatory pathways, the high GI drives insulin-mediated inflammation, and acrylamide formed during high-temperature frying is an additional inflammatory concern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mr Potato Original Flavour Potato Crisps is a mass-market single-serve snack manufactured by Calbee-Mamee Sdn Bhd, a joint venture between Calbee (Japan) and Mamee-Double Decker (Malaysia), and is the dominant potato crisp brand in both Malaysia and Singapore. Each single-serve pack weighs 55 g and is ubiquitous in school canteens, 7-Eleven convenience stores, provision shops, and supermarket snack aisles across Singapore. Unlike extruded potato snacks such as Piattos, Mr Potato Original uses actual sliced potatoes that are continuously fried in palm olein and seasoned with a simple salt, onion powder, and garlic powder blend, giving a classic savoury, lightly garlicky flavour with a crisp, thin texture. Nutritionally, the product is energy-dense at 527 kcal/100 g (approximately 290 kcal per pack), with 30 g fat/100 g — of which 14 g is saturated fat from palm olein — and 62 g carbohydrate/100 g. A distinctive nutritional feature is the high potassium content (850 mg/100 g; 467 mg per pack), which is genuine potato-derived potassium and unusual for a processed snack. Sodium is 545 mg/100 g (300 mg per pack). The glycemic index is approximately 75 (high), yielding a glycemic load of ~26 per 55 g pack. The product is certified halal, is vegan and gluten-free, and contains no cholesterol. Clinically, it is a high-concern food for hypertension (high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high GI and GL), and cardiovascular disease (high saturated fat and sodium). It is classified as ultra-processed under NOVA classification.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from the verified Calbee-Mamee Singapore product NIP (Nutrition Information Panel) on the product label, which is an approved primary source under Project Veritas v2 source policy for packaged commercial products. Micronutrient values cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 19411, plain salted potato chips). GI sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for fried potato crisps — an approved GI source. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and GI values are fully supported.
notes: trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per the product NIP (0 g trans fat declared). cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero — this is a plant-based fried snack with no animal-derived ingredients contributing cholesterol. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are confirmed zero per product label and consistent with USDA FDC data for plain potato chips. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1.0: detailed fatty acid breakdown beyond saturated fat is not declared on the Singapore product NIP and is not available in the USDA FDC SR Legacy entry for this specific product formulation. water_g is set to -1.0: moisture content is not declared on the product NIP and is not available from the approved sources consulted for this specific branded product. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for regular fried potato crisps; no direct GI test of Mr Potato Original brand has been published in peer-reviewed literature as of April 2026. Glycemic load per 55 g serving calculated as GL = (75 × 34.1) / 100 = 25.6, where available carbohydrate = 62.0 g/100 g × 0.55 = 34.1 g. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301710 and FoodEx2 code A00LC are closest-parent matches; no branded potato crisp codes exist in either ontology. Aliases consistency self-check performed (iteration 3): all 20 string values in aliases_by_language verified present verbatim in flat aliases array — english (9 entries), chinese (4 entries), malay (2 entries), tamil (2 entries), hindi (1 entry), romanized (2 entries). No discrepancies found.
